programme.desc: Dom Littlewood and Michelle Ackerley with a report on the counterfeit contact lenses that can make your eyesight worse, and the fake waste disposal companies that could cost consumers a fortune. Elsewhere, Michelle shows how to identify fake emails and dangerous links online, and reveals how criminals can even embed fake messages into a legitimate text chain on your phone(n)

programme.title: Shop Well for Less?
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Steph McGovern are in Milton Keynes to meet the Howarth family - one of the biggest spending families to ever feature on the programme - to help them change their shopping habits in an attempt to save money. A lack of time management means the family has slid into some bad shopping habits, often buying two or three of the same household items, and having recently renovated their home, the family find themselves with credit card debt. Can Alex and Steph get this family to rein in their spending and get on top of their debt?(n)

programme.title: Sacred Wonders
programme.desc: What people do for faith in some of the most stunning sacred places on Earth. In the second episode, a Shinto devotee undertakes a gruelling challenge at the sacred Nachi Falls in Japan, hoping to ensure the wellbeing of his country for another year. Meanwhile in Mali, a young Muslim man helps to re-plaster Djenne's Great Mosque in the hope of a place in paradise, and at the Cathedral of St John the Divine in New York, an Anglican priest manages an astonishing ceremony to bring people - and their animals - closer to God(n)

programme.desc: The aftermath of the devastating fire has left a blackened smoking wasteland, but rain soon brings hope to the scorched earth. Kike the cheetah and wild dog leader Jasari are both teaching their cubs how to survive on their own, only to be interrupted by a grieving giraffe. A baby elephant struggles in the flooded river, while Bakari returns when his family are threatened by a rival baboon troop. Lioness Kali has surprises in store which bring hope for the future of her pride, and as things draw to a close, a death brings all the families together in a remarkable truce(n)

programme.desc: Metropolitan Police's Extradition Unit search for two people convicted of slavery and extortion offences in Italy, but DS Pete Rance and his team decide to take a cautious approach as the pair have a history of violence. programme.title: Fake or Fortune?
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and Philip Mould investigate the provenance of a painting with a Venetian view, trying to ascertain whether it is the work of Francesco Guardi or Michele Marieschi. Owner Nick Hopkinson inherited the painting from his great grandfather and Guardi and Marieschi are both known for their depictions of Venetian views and were contemporaries in the 18th century of Canaletto. But there's a big difference in value between them - if Nick's painting is a Marieschi it could be worth half a million pounds, if it's a Guardi it could be worth up to £10million(n)

programme.desc: Oscar-winning actress Kate Winslet follows a rumour of Scandinavian ancestry on her late mother Sally's side of the family and is delighted when it turns out to be true and that she has Swedish heritage. Switching to her father's side of the family, Kate finds a drummer boy in the Grenadier Guards and, this time, an unusually positive encounter with prison(n)

programme.desc: After a career serving all over the world, army veteran Jason, his wife Esther, and their four children have finally put down roots - but the trouble is their Suffolk garden is little more than a concrete car park. As the military is a big part of this family's life, they want to see the resulting garden incorporate some remembrance poppies. Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich head to Stowmarket to see how they can help Jason and Esther to realise their dreams(n)

programme.desc: Edith and Phil are determined to fulfill their dream of retiring to the sun together, but find themselves continually hampered by logistical difficulties. Edith is finding her irritating sister Joan very difficult to accommodate, and Roger, impatient to see Phil sell his house, decides to help the process along by checking his roof for dry rot. Wendy drops in on Edith and mentions that she is planning a children's book, and despite not yet knowing what the plotline will be, thinks it may involve a complicated family. programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: A 79-year-old woman with dementia runs away from her care home and turns up at a south London police station to report a murder - sparking a reinvestigation into the mysterious disappearance of her police officer husband in 1956. The official report says that he fell into the river after one too many beers - but what secrets was this seemingly straight-as-a die old-fashioned bobby keeping? Meanwhile, Steve's son is arrested for buying weed, bringing the retired cop face to face with his ex-wife - a woman he hasn't seen in 10 years. Tamzin Outhwaite and Denis Lawson star, with a guest appearance from Julie Graham(n)

programme.title: The Cruise: A Life at Sea
programme.desc: The Balmoral leaves Dubai as it sets off on the last leg of its world tour, stopping briefly in Oman before heading through the Suez Canal to the Egyptian port of Alexandria. The Rev Colin Still and a group of passengers visit the Allied war cemetery at El Alamein to commemorate those that fell in the battle. On board, people come together to take part in the Country Fayre to raise money for charity, and then it's time for end-of-cruise shows by the passengers and staff, who bid tearful goodbyes at the end of the four-month voyage(n)

programme.title: Animal Park Summer Special
programme.desc: Ben Fogle and Kate Humble meet two endangered Cuban crocodiles, while keepers and vets are battling to get to the bottom of why rare a red panda is not putting on weight(n)

programme.title: Animal Babies: First Year on Earth
programme.desc: Cameras follow six baby animals as they learn the basic skills needed to face the challenges of surviving their first year on Earth. In Kenya, new-born elephant calf Safina learns how to keep up with her fast moving herd, and in Sri Lanka, one-week-old Jazir has to quickly learn the ropes of a primate society. At the edge of the Arctic, five-week-old fox cubs must learn to hunt before their siblings beat them to limited food, and in Uganda, an eight-week-old mountain gorilla adapts to forest life(n)

programme.desc: Vet Rosie treats a concussed tawny owl, while equine specialist Kerry helps a horse with a badly cut face. Meanwhile, in Fife, Adam has an emotional case on his hands when an owner suffering from a debilitating phobia discovers the dog he depends on has a worrying lump. Narrated by Felicity Kendal(n)

programme.title: Extraordinary Animals
programme.desc: Hugh Fearnley-Whittingstall and a team of wildlife experts focus on animals' relationships with each other and with humans. Hugh discovers how the capuchin monkeys of Costa Rica have such a fantastic team spirit, biologist Patrick Aryee investigates man's relationship with the vulture - nature's most infamous scavenger - and zoologist Lucy Cooke finds out about the medicine in Kentucky that may improve a rare pig's relationship problems. Conservationist Giles Clark ventures into the rainforests of Thailand to find out how a clever matchmaking technique could help save the clouded leopard from extinction(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team bring four treasured family heirlooms back to life. Ceramics conservator Kirsten Ramsay is charged with the task of repairing a little cat teapot with an extraordinary wartime story behind it, while Dominic Chinea takes on the restoration of a once brightly coloured wooden carousel horse. The team also brings a wooden boat puzzle and a Victorian magic lantern back to life(n)

programme.title: Interior Design Masters

programme.title: The Chefs' Brigade
programme.desc: Jason Atherton takes the chefs to Cadiz in Southern Spain, a city famous for its seafood. The brigade must cook for a restaurant that has been run by the same family for 50 years, and is adored by locals and tourists alike. Jason must encourage the chefs to appreciate local traditions, and arranges for a local fisherman to take them to the salt flats that have provided seafood to residents for thousands of years. Back in the kitchen, several members of the brigade have to win back Jason's trust following recent confrontations(n)

programme.title: Remarkable Places to Eat
programme.desc: Leading chefs take maitre d' and First Dates star Fred Sirieix to experience dining at their favourite restaurants and explore what goes into making them so special. This time, chef Michel Roux Jr takes Fred Sirieix to some of his favourite restaurants in Paris. First, the pair visit one of the most famous restaurants in the city, La Tour D'Argent, where they experience a signature dish that has been on the menu since 1890. Moving away from restaurants that offer expensive indulgence, Michel takes Fred to a place reviving one of the simplest and cheapest French classics - the ham baguette. For the final stop of their Parisian trip, the pair visit Brasserie La Couple, which boasts the biggest dining room in Paris(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jonathan Owen from Sussex arrives at the barn, bearing a small item with a big history for the attention of woodwork specialist Will Kirk. The prized possession is a hand-made model Spitfire plane, carved by Jonathan's Uncle Reggie between bombing raids over Germany. Master saddlemaker Suzie Fletcher tackles the repair of a worn out and badly torn leather pouffe with precious memories for its owner Lou, silversmith Brenton West takes on the restoration of a battered silver purse and mechanical guru Steve Fletcher is faced with how to carry out the delicate repair of an old Victorian rain gauge(n)

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: Raymond and a team of volunteers give the valais blacknose sheep a bath, Kirsty and Tom rescue fallen sheep on the isle, and Stevie attempts to milk his first buffalo. In Shetland, Kirsty and Tom again row out to St Ninian's Isle to attempt to rescue a ewe and a lamb that have fallen from the isle's steep cliffs(n)

programme.title: Murder Case
programme.desc: The police's number-one suspect in the case of murdered 47-year-old Julie Reilly is interrogated, but the interview leads to frustration for Julie's family and the detectives(n)

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.sub-title: Joe Lycett, Ivo Graham, Phil Wang
programme.desc: Stand-up comedian and author Joe Lycett plays host in this show recorded before a packed house at London's Hammersmith Apollo. He warms up the audience and then introduces routines by fellow funny men Ivo Graham and Phil Wang(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Mark Urban(n)

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: No Details Available(n)

programme.title: Interior Design Masters
programme.desc: Aspiring designers take on commercial interior challenges, as they compete to win a life changing contract. Presented by Fearne Cotton. In the first episode, the ten contestants are split into two teams and must redesign two show homes, working together to deliver a cohesive end product that impresses the judges and is accessible to prospective buyers(n)

programme.title: How to Break into the Elite
programme.desc: Amol Rajan investigates how much class still matters in Britain's leading professions, finding out what it takes for young working-class people to break into the elite as he spends time with graduates looking for prestigious positions. He explores how media industry's mystifying cultural codes provide an obstacle to many candidates from disadvantaged backgrounds, and looks at the link between confidence and class(n)

programme.title: Our Cops in the North
programme.desc: Observational documentary following the work of Northumbria Police across Newcastle, Sunderland and beyond, telling stories of the force's officers and victims of crime. With top-to-bottom access to the force, the series follows crimes from murder to motorbike theft and poaching to pub fights, as call handlers, urgent responders, detectives and commanders work around the clock to protect the public and solve their cases. It goes beyond the adrenaline-fuelled blue lights, to give profound insight into the real experience of those fighting crime and those who become victims of it(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Raj Bisram and Stephanie Connell help two teams scour Malvern antiques and collectors fair to pick up items to sell at auction, while host Eric Knowles meets an Action Man collector to learn more about this much-loved toy(n)

programme.title: Animal Park Summer Special
programme.desc: The keepers try to stop Red the Amur tiger from launching terrifying attacks on visitors' cars and Jean Johansson helps to keep the mischievous macaques out of trouble(n)

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don demonstrates how to produce a medley of vegetables from a small space. He also lifts and divides irises and adds spires of summer colour to his borders. Frances Tophill visits an allotment on the Isle of Man where plants are grown and harvested as ingredients for cosmetics, while Adam Frost is in Northamptonshire finding out about the design and planting at Cottesbrooke Hall. Plus, an interview with a woman whose passion for bees is inspiring young beekeepers at a school in Huddersfield(n)

programme.title: BBC News at 9
programme.desc: No Details Available(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Curious Creatures
programme.desc: Ferne Corrigan and Iolo Williams join team captains Chris Packham and Lucy Cooke on the panel show that tests wildlife knowledge, with Kate Humble asking the questions(n)

programme.title: Getting the Builders In
programme.desc: Elizabeth and Sean need builders to repair a DIY bodge job on their fireplace in Stretford, while first-time buyer Sam wants his tired kitchen in Battersea transforming(n)

programme.title: Len and Ainsley's Big Food Adventure
programme.desc: Chef Ainsley Harriott and Strictly Come Dancing judge Len Goodman's road trip takes the pair to south London, where Len has his first taste of Portuguese and Japanese cuisine. Ainsley heads to the kitchen and cooks a mouth-watering Portuguese chicken inspired by the day's adventures(n)

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers explore the popularity of takeaway food in Britain and discuss the various ways that it has evolved over the years. The culinary duo also rustle up some of their favourite dishes(n)

programme.title: More Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: Equine vet Sybil has to contend with a Highland cow in Teesdale, Co Durham, while treating Fell ponies, and Steve judges a dog competition at the local Bowes Agricultural Show. Meanwhile, Angela has to break bad news to the owner of a Rottweiler at Fife's small animal hospital. Narrated by Felicity Kendal(n)

programme.title: Extraordinary Animals
programme.desc: Hugh Fearnley-Whittingstall and a team of wildlife experts explore creatures' surprising physical characteristics, including how one man's understanding of an eagle's physical abilities could help save it from extinction. Conservationist Giles Clark witnesses an Australian kangaroo that is more at home climbing trees than hopping through the Outback, zoologist Lucy Cooke is in Costa Rica to find out if the sloth's laziness is the key to its success, and marine biologist Patrick Aryee reveals the secrets of one of the world's oddest looking creatures - the platypus(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Philip Serrell and Louise Gostelow head to the final auction in Brighton. Louise ends up in a beret and, with brush in hand, recreates a very old form of English art. Meanwhile, dog lover Phil learns about the history of some the country's favourite felines. At the last auction, which will attract the biggest bid - Phil's silver boxer dog or Louise's bronze puppy?(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Eggheads
programme.desc: Anna Williamson, Gavin Ramjaun, Martel Maxwell, Rav Wilding and Paul Rose form another celebrity team aiming to triumph over the Eggheads. Presented by Jeremy Vine(n)

programme.title: The Customer Is Always Right
programme.desc: The customers unbox, test and score a baby bib and tray kit, a smart mirror and a seaweed oil. But only one can win the Customer is Always Right trophy(n)

programme.title: Monkman & Seagull's Genius Guide to Britain
programme.desc: Eric Monkman and Bobby Seagull head to Wales and discover a strong industrial engineering history, sailing across Pontcysyllte Aqueduct, the highest navigable aqueduct in the world. The tiny village of Hawarden gives the duo the opportunity to stay overnight in the UK's only residential library, before they make an attempt on the summit of Mount Snowdon itself - via the mountain's iconic rack-and-pinion narrow gauge steam railway train(n)

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys invites more contestants to brave the black chair to answer question on specialised subjects including the Mughal - or Mogul - Empire, the life and legacy of physician and suffragist Elizabeth Garrett Anderson and Rod Serling's sci-fi series The Twilight Zone. They are then each afforded two minutes to demonstrate their prowess on general knowledge(n)

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Bob Mortimer has harboured a childhood dream to catch a salmon, so together with friend Paul Whitehouse, he sets off for the River Tay in Scotland full of hope and excitement. A ghillie guides them up the intimidating river in a boat and they fish using various methods to catch the elusive fish. They stay in the heart of the Highlands, where Bob cooks Paul a traditional Scottish breakfast and enjoy a swift round of golf before resuming their fishing. The duo also reflect on luck and fate and the part it has played in their lives and later assess whether the trip has lived up to their expectations(n)

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: As Longmeadow radiates colour in the height of summer, Monty Don takes a range of cuttings to get plants for free and also shows how best to prune lavender. Carol Klein visits a garden in Devon to revel in its eryngiums, while the programme also meets a passionate gardener and his partner in Wiltshire who use their garden as inspiration for art(n)

programme.title: Fosse/Verdon
programme.desc: The year 1973 finds Bob juggling dealing with the success of Cabaret, spearheading production on Liza with a Z and Pippin and grappling with the price of fame. Though he is indulging in the high life, with several girlfriends, pills and poppers, he finds time to attend the opening night of Gwen's Broadway play Children! Children! Gwen tearfully tells him that the play is closing after only one performance, since the producers have had word of bad notices and poor box office. She implies that if Bob had helped with rewrites, the production might have been saved. Sam Rockwell and Michelle Williams star(n)

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Matt Lucas, Holly Walsh and Susan Calman join regular panellist Alan Davies, as questionmaster Sandi Toksvig opens her post in a post-themed edition. As ever, correctness and even intelligence go out of the window as the panellists attempt to answer the fiendishly difficult questions, with points invariably awarded for the answers the questionmaster finds most interesting, as opposed to correct(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Mark Urban(n)

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: No Details Available(n)

programme.title: Edinburgh Nights with Nish Kumar
programme.desc: The comedian, actor, and presenter goes behind the scenes to sample The Blind Boys of Alabama in a remarkable partnership with Malian duo Amadou and Mariam. My Land, the Hungarian show that was top-rated of all the shows at Edinburgh last year, lets Nish behind the scenes to see the participants on the high wires pushing the boundary of what the human body is capable of. Nish also gets to know some of this year's up-and-coming comedians(n)

programme.title: In the Heat of the Night
programme.desc: When a wealthy industrialist is murdered in Mississippi, the bigoted local sheriff is quick to accuse a black newcomer to town - only to learn his suspect is a highly respected homicide detective from Philadelphia. However, the duo are forced to put aside their mutual loathing and team up to track down the killer. Oscar-winning drama, starring Sidney Poitier, Rod Steiger and Warren Oates(n)

credits.actor: Sidney Poitier, Rod Steiger, Warren Oates

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: Eisteddfod 2019 with Jason Mohammad
programme.desc: Part one of two. The presenter reports from Llanrwst in the Conwy Valley, bringing highlights and stories from the National Eisteddfod of Wales. He catches up with Welsh rugby stars Jonathan Davies and Ken Owens, as well as broadcaster and comedian Tudur Owen, who are all being honoured at the Eisteddfod this year(n)

programme.title: A History of Scotland
programme.desc: Historian Neil Oliver explores the roles played by Alexander II, who forged the country through blood and violence, and William Wallace, a patriot whose resistance to King Edward of England inspired Scottish people to fight for their independence(n)

programme.title: The First Georgians: The German Kings Who Made Britain
programme.desc: Lucy Worsley investigates how George II's family fought the French, the Jacobites and one another at the same time. She examines his troubles with his son Frederick, Prince of Wales, and the way he led from the front on the battlefield, becoming the last British monarch to do so, and helped turn the country into a global superpower(n)

programme.title: Mary Beard's Ultimate Rome: Empire Without Limit
programme.desc: Scholar Mary Beard tackles one of the biggest puzzles of all as she examines how and when the Roman Empire fell, surveying the walls and fortifications of Britain and Germany. She seeks to redefine understanding of so-called 'Barbarian Invasions', before ultimately asking whether the Empire was transformed rather than destroyed, and still lives on in the world today(n)

programme.title: Susan Calman's Fringe Benefits
programme.desc: The comedian introduces highlights from the Edinburgh Fringe, showcasing some of this year's best new talent. She chats to special guests including Frank Skinner, Larry Dean, Iain Stirling and Desiree Burch, and introduces performances by Jo Caulfield and Stuart Mitchell(n)

programme.title: Timeshift
programme.sub-title: Timeshift: The Last Days of Steam
programme.desc: Between 1948 and 1960, governments around the world invested in electric tracks and diesel, but in Britain hundreds of steam-powered trains were built. This film looks at the reasons behind their continued production, and asks what happened to them(n)

programme.title: Calculating Ada: The Countess of Computing
programme.desc: Hannah Fry tells the story of Ada Lovelace, the daughter of Lord Byron and a scandalous socialite. But when Charles Babbage designed the world's first steam-powered computers, most famously the analytical engine, it was Ada who realised the full potential of these machines(n)

programme.title: A History of Scotland
programme.desc: Historian Neil Oliver explores the roles played by Alexander II, who forged the country through blood and violence, and William Wallace, a patriot whose resistance to King Edward of England inspired Scottish people to fight for their independence(n)

programme.title: The First Georgians: The German Kings Who Made Britain
programme.desc: Lucy Worsley investigates how George II's family fought the French, the Jacobites and one another at the same time. She examines his troubles with his son Frederick, Prince of Wales, and the way he led from the front on the battlefield, becoming the last British monarch to do so, and helped turn the country into a global superpower(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: The Sky at Night
programme.desc: The programme goes behind the scenes as the European Space Agency selects its next mission, which will be launched in 2028, meeting British teams vying to have their ideas selected, including a revolutionary mission to a comet(n)

programme.title: Skies Above Britain
programme.desc: With several recent tragedies at air shows, two former RAF pilots - the Wildcats - must finish their display season safely. Meanwhile, a champion skydiver juggles motherhood with an intense training schedule, and a man attempts to fly Britain's first solar balloon(n)

programme.title: Operation Wild
programme.desc: Clare Balding travels to South Africa to join a veterinary team trying to help a rhino called Thandi who was attacked by poachers for her horn. For the first time, a plastic surgeon will attempt a skin graft on the animal to heal the wound. She also visits the rainforest of Borneo to follow the story of an orangutan who needs cataracts removing from her eyes so that she can be released back into the wild. Elsewhere, vet Steve Leonard takes a trip to Chicago's Lincoln Park Zoo, where the latest technology is being used to help chimpanzees with heart disease and visits the South African city of Pretoria, where a Cape fur seal requires a CT scan to identify the root cause of her illness, before witnessing a giraffe being anaesthetised to treat a leg injury(n)

programme.title: From Ice to Fire: The Incredible Science of Temperature
programme.desc: Physicist Dr Helen Czerski explores the narrow band of temperature that has led to life on Earth. She reveals how life began in a place where hot meets cold, and how every living creature on Earth depends on temperature for its survival. She uncovers the extraordinary natural engineering that animals have evolved to keep their bodies at the right temperature and she witnesses the remarkable surgery that is using temperature to take the human body to the extremes(n)

programme.title: Immortal Egypt with Joann Fletcher
programme.desc: The professor explores one of Saqqara's last pyramid complexes to illustrate how Ancient Egypt's 'Pyramid Age' came to an end. A worsening climate combined with political upheaval, famine and economic difficulties to plunge the state into a dark era of civil war, with the land dividing into smaller city-states headed by ambitious small-town leaders. In an obscure tomb in Thebes, she uncovers the stories of warriors who fought in the bloody battle that would eventually lead to Egypt's reunification, and reveals how settlers known as the 'Hyksos' tried to infiltrate the fledgling nation's government to seize the throne(n)

programme.title: Constable: A Country Rebel
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the innovation behind John Constable's art, which was considered truly groundbreaking in its era. Ahead of a new exhibition at the V&A Museum in London, presenter Alastair Sooke focuses on his painting The Haywain, and reveals how Constable struggled to achieve success in Britain during his lifetime, despite being revered in France(n)

programme.title: Unsung Heroines: Danielle de Niese on the Lost World of Female Composers
programme.desc: The soprano explores the lives and works of five female composers, from the middle ages to the late 20th century, who were famous in their lifetimes but whose work was then forgotten. Traditionally, western classical music is seen as a procession of male geniuses, but the truth is that women have always composed. Hildegard of Bingen, Francesca Caccini, Clara Schumann, Florence Price and Elizabeth Maconchy all battled to fulfil their ambitions and overcome the obstacles that society placed in their way, but then disappeared into obscurity and only some have found recognition again. Contributors include pianist Lucy Parham(n)

programme.title: A Brief History of Graffiti
programme.desc: Dr Richard Clay traces the development of graffiti over 30,000 years, from the prehistoric cave-paintings found in Burgundy, France, through to the gladiatorial fan-worship of Roman Lyon, and the messages left on the walls of the German Reichstag by Russian troops in 1945. Along the way, he unpicks the many reasons that compel people to scribble and scratch lasting mementos to their successors into any available surface, and reveals how these rudimentary memorials have developed into a legitimate art-form(n)

programme.title: From Ice to Fire: The Incredible Science of Temperature
programme.desc: Physicist Dr Helen Czerski explores the narrow band of temperature that has led to life on Earth. She reveals how life began in a place where hot meets cold, and how every living creature on Earth depends on temperature for its survival. She uncovers the extraordinary natural engineering that animals have evolved to keep their bodies at the right temperature and she witnesses the remarkable surgery that is using temperature to take the human body to the extremes(n)

programme.title: World News Today
programme.desc: The day's leading stories(n)

programme.title: BBC Proms 2019
programme.sub-title: BBC Proms: Pappano and the National Youth Orchestra USA
programme.desc: Suzy Klein introduces America's most talented young musicians in an ambitious concert from the National Youth Orchestra of the USA. Antonio Pappano conducts the UK premiere of Benjamin Beckman's new work, Occidentalis and mezzo-soprano Joyce DiDonato sings Berlioz's beautifully crafted miniatures Les nuits d'été. The impressive programme culminates with Richard Strauss's epic work, An Alpine Symphony(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1988
programme.desc: Mike Read and Simon Mayo introduce the edition first aired on May 12, featuring performances by Harry Enfield, Prince, the Adventures, Narada, Liverpool FC, Belinda Carlisle, Derek B, Prefab Sprout, Star Turn on 45 Pints, Wet Wet Wet, Fairground Attraction and Kylie Minogue(n)

programme.title: Woodstock - Three Days That Defined a Generation
programme.desc: In August 1969, over 400,000 people from all walks of life converged on a small dairy farm in upstate New York to hear a pop concert. However, most experienced something far more profound - a moment that came to define a cultural revolution. Through the voices of those who were there, the music of the time and unique imagery shot in situ of the protagonists and festivalgoers, this documentary tells the story of the lead up to those three historic days, and those extraordinary moments when the concert, almost pulled off right at the last minute, put the ideals of the counterculture to the test(n)

programme.title: Jimi Hendrix: The Road to Woodstock
programme.desc: A definitive account of one of the guitarist's most celebrated performances, at the New York festival in 1969, featuring signature renditions of Purple Haze, Voodoo Child and the Star Spangled Banner. The programme includes interviews with Woodstock promoter Michael Lang and Hendrix's band members Mitch Mitchell, Billy Cox, Larry Lee and Juma Sultan(n)

programme.title: Millie Inbetween
programme.desc: Dad and Amber are back together - and Amber's moving in. It's only temporary, until they find a bigger place, but the resulting chaos drives Millie, Lauren, Fran and Jake round to Mum's. And Millie has an ulterior motive: getting Craig to look after the hyper Jake as revenge for his annoying behaviour. She thinks it'll teach him to be a better brother - but Jake thinks Craig is pretty cool as he is, and starts imitating his teenage ways. programme.title: Shop Well for Less?
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Steph McGovern are in Milton Keynes to meet the Howarth family - one of the biggest spending families to ever feature on the programme - to help them change their shopping habits in an attempt to save money. A lack of time management means the family has slid into some bad shopping habits, often buying two or three of the same household items, and having recently renovated their home, the family find themselves with credit card debt. Can Alex and Steph get this family to rein in their spending and get on top of their debt?(n)

programme.title: Sacred Wonders

Meanwhile in Mali, a young Muslim man helps to re-plaster Djenne's Great Mosque in the hope of a place in paradise, and at the Cathedral of St John the Divine in New York, an Anglican priest manages an astonishing ceremony to bring people - and their animals - closer to God(n)

programme.title: Serengeti

Kike the cheetah and wild dog leader Jasari are both teaching their cubs how to survive on their own, only to be interrupted by a grieving giraffe. A baby elephant struggles in the flooded river, while Bakari returns when his family are threatened by a rival baboon troop. Lioness Kali has surprises in store which bring hope for the future of her pride, and as things draw to a close, a death brings all the families together in a remarkable truce(n)

programme.title: Fake or Fortune?
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and Philip Mould investigate the provenance of a painting with a Venetian view, trying to ascertain whether it is the work of Francesco Guardi or Michele Marieschi. Owner Nick Hopkinson inherited the painting from his great grandfather and Guardi and Marieschi are both known for their depictions of Venetian views and were contemporaries in the 18th century of Canaletto. But there's a big difference in value between them - if Nick's painting is a Marieschi it could be worth half a million pounds, if it's a Guardi it could be worth up to £10million(n)

programme.title: Gareth Thomas v Homophobia: The Legacy
programme.desc: The former rugby international continues his campaign to wipe out homophobia in football. With reported incidents of homophobic abuse at football matches at a record high, Gareth enlists the help of parliament as he attempts to outlaw abuse in stadia. He also recruits Cardiff City, Leeds United and Norwich City in his campaign to tackle homophobia in the boardroom, the dressing room and on the terraces. All of this while coping with being the victim of a violent homophobic attack himself(n)

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Oscar-winning actress Kate Winslet follows a rumour of Scandinavian ancestry on her late mother Sally's side of the family and is delighted when it turns out to be true and that she has Swedish heritage. However, her joy turns to tears and anger when she uncovers the extreme hardships her ancestors endured, from famine to flogging and imprisonment. Switching to her father's side of the family, Kate finds a drummer boy in the Grenadier Guards and, this time, an unusually positive encounter with prison(n)

programme.title: Weatherman Walking
programme.desc: Derek Brockway embarks on a couple more rambling routes around Wales. First he tries out barefoot walking in the hills above Crickhowell with Lynne Allbutt - the first person to run barefoot across Wales. Then he walks around Bala Lake - the biggest lake in Wales - partly on the route of Mari Jones, who in 1800 walked 25 miles barefoot to buy a copy of the bible in Welsh(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Stuart is alarmed to find Mick struggling to breathe and collapsed on the floor, while Max confronts Bobby for questioning him about Lucy. Sheree continues to make herself at home at the salon and suggests entering a hairdressing competition, much to Denise's annoyance(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Brighton & Hove Albion striker Glenn Murray, Great Britain sprinter Harry Aikines-Aryeetey, Olympic swimming medallist Sharron Davies and three-time world snooker champion Mark Selby join host Sue Barker and team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell on the quiz(n)

programme.title: Hold the Sunset
programme.desc: Edith and Phil are determined to fulfill their dream of retiring to the sun together, but find themselves continually hampered by logistical difficulties. Edith is finding her irritating sister Joan very difficult to accommodate, and Roger, impatient to see Phil sell his house, decides to help the process along by checking his roof for dry rot. Wendy drops in on Edith and mentions that she is planning a children's book, and despite not yet knowing what the plotline will be, thinks it may involve a complicated family. Comedy, starring Alison Steadman, Jason Watkins, John Cleese and Sue Johnston(n)

credits.actor: Alison Steadman, Jason Watkins, John Cleese, Sue Johnston

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: A secretive phone call from a solicitor to Mrs Brown leaves her children suspecting one of them may be adopted, while Father Quinn returns from rehab and asks Agnes to help out at his annual garden party - although as she proceeds to drive him crazy with a smartphone, his recovery may be short-lived. Meanwhile, Cathy receives a blast from the past, Rory and Dino have a bust-up, and Mark and Betty deliver bad news - they are leaving for Australia right away. Domestic comedy, starring Brendan O'Carroll(n)

programme.title: Fake Britain
programme.desc: Dom Littlewood and Michelle Ackerley with a report on the counterfeit contact lenses that can make your eyesight worse, and the fake waste disposal companies that could cost consumers a fortune. Elsewhere, Michelle shows how to identify fake emails and dangerous links online, and reveals how criminals can even embed fake messages into a legitimate text chain on your phone(n)

programme.title: Shop Well for Less?
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Steph McGovern are in Milton Keynes to meet the Howarth family - one of the biggest spending families to ever feature on the programme - to help them change their shopping habits in an attempt to save money. A lack of time management means the family has slid into some bad shopping habits, often buying two or three of the same household items, and having recently renovated their home, the family find themselves with credit card debt. Can Alex and Steph get this family to rein in their spending and get on top of their debt?(n)

programme.title: Sacred Wonders
programme.desc: What people do for faith in some of the most stunning sacred places on Earth. In the second episode, a Shinto devotee undertakes a gruelling challenge at the sacred Nachi Falls in Japan, hoping to ensure the wellbeing of his country for another year. Meanwhile in Mali, a young Muslim man helps to re-plaster Djenne's Great Mosque in the hope of a place in paradise, and at the Cathedral of St John the Divine in New York, an Anglican priest manages an astonishing ceremony to bring people - and their animals - closer to God(n)

programme.title: The Warwick Uni Rape Chat Scandal
programme.desc: Documentary about the scandal of online rape threats made by students in 2018, featuring contributions made from those involved in the case and examining the decision to allow some of the men involved back on campus, despite the original intention to bar them for 10 years(n)

programme.title: Serengeti
programme.desc: The aftermath of the devastating fire has left a blackened smoking wasteland, but rain soon brings hope to the scorched earth. Kike the cheetah and wild dog leader Jasari are both teaching their cubs how to survive on their own, only to be interrupted by a grieving giraffe. A baby elephant struggles in the flooded river, while Bakari returns when his family are threatened by a rival baboon troop. Lioness Kali has surprises in store which bring hope for the future of her pride, and as things draw to a close, a death brings all the families together in a remarkable truce(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Sonia anxiously waits to hear news of Bex's A-level results and Stuart makes a desperate plea to win Mick back as a friend, but his words fall on deaf ears. After an unsettling conversation with Iqra, Honey tells Will and Janet to pack their things, while Iqra apologises for lying to Mariam and Arshad(n)

programme.title: Fugitives
programme.desc: Metropolitan Police's Extradition Unit search for two people convicted of slavery and extortion offences in Italy, but DS Pete Rance and his team decide to take a cautious approach as the pair have a history of violence. In Derbyshire, the team are on the road before dawn looking for a man wanted in Slovakia, while detectives in south Wales work with Tanzanian police to locate a murderer on the run in Africa(n)

programme.title: Fake or Fortune?
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and Philip Mould investigate the provenance of a painting with a Venetian view, trying to ascertain whether it is the work of Francesco Guardi or Michele Marieschi. Owner Nick Hopkinson inherited the painting from his great grandfather and Guardi and Marieschi are both known for their depictions of Venetian views and were contemporaries in the 18th century of Canaletto. But there's a big difference in value between them - if Nick's painting is a Marieschi it could be worth half a million pounds, if it's a Guardi it could be worth up to £10million(n)

programme.title: Scotland's Home of the Year
programme.desc: Architect and university lecturer Michael Angus, Glasgow-based interior designer Anna Campbell-Jones and globally recognised lifestyle blogger Kate Spiers visit three homes in the east of Scotland. programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: A 79-year-old woman with dementia runs away from her care home and turns up at a south London police station to report a murder - sparking a reinvestigation into the mysterious disappearance of her police officer husband in 1956. The official report says that he fell into the river after one too many beers - but what secrets was this seemingly straight-as-a die old-fashioned bobby keeping? Meanwhile, Steve's son is arrested for buying weed, bringing the retired cop face to face with his ex-wife - a woman he hasn't seen in 10 years. Tamzin Outhwaite and Denis Lawson star, with a guest appearance from Julie Graham(n)

programme.title: Serengeti
programme.desc: The aftermath of the devastating fire has left a blackened smoking wasteland, but rain soon brings hope to the scorched earth. Kike the cheetah and wild dog leader Jasari are both teaching their cubs how to survive on their own, only to be interrupted by a grieving giraffe. A baby elephant struggles in the flooded river, while Bakari returns when his family are threatened by a rival baboon troop. Lioness Kali has surprises in store which bring hope for the future of her pride, and as things draw to a close, a death brings all the families together in a remarkable truce(n)

programme.title: Fugitives
programme.desc: Metropolitan Police's Extradition Unit search for two people convicted of slavery and extortion offences in Italy, but DS Pete Rance and his team decide to take a cautious approach as the pair have a history of violence. In Derbyshire, the team are on the road before dawn looking for a man wanted in Slovakia, while detectives in south Wales work with Tanzanian police to locate a murderer on the run in Africa(n)

programme.title: Fake or Fortune?
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and Philip Mould investigate the provenance of a painting with a Venetian view, trying to ascertain whether it is the work of Francesco Guardi or Michele Marieschi. Owner Nick Hopkinson inherited the painting from his great grandfather and Guardi and Marieschi are both known for their depictions of Venetian views and were contemporaries in the 18th century of Canaletto. But there's a big difference in value between them - if Nick's painting is a Marieschi it could be worth half a million pounds, if it's a Guardi it could be worth up to £10million(n)

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Oscar-winning actress Kate Winslet follows a rumour of Scandinavian ancestry on her late mother Sally's side of the family and is delighted when it turns out to be true and that she has Swedish heritage. However, her joy turns to tears and anger when she uncovers the extreme hardships her ancestors endured, from famine to flogging and imprisonment. Switching to her father's side of the family, Kate finds a drummer boy in the Grenadier Guards and, this time, an unusually positive encounter with prison(n)

programme.desc: After a career serving all over the world, army veteran Jason, his wife Esther, and their four children have finally put down roots - but the trouble is their Suffolk garden is little more than a concrete car park. The couple have a budget of £3,000 to try to create a space where they can enjoy sitting out as a family. As the military is a big part of this family's life, they want to see the resulting garden incorporate some remembrance poppies. Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich head to Stowmarket to see how they can help Jason and Esther to realise their dreams(n)

Edith is finding her irritating sister Joan very difficult to accommodate, and Roger, impatient to see Phil sell his house, decides to help the process along by checking his roof for dry rot. Wendy drops in on Edith and mentions that she is planning a children's book, and despite not yet knowing what the plotline will be, thinks it may involve a complicated family. programme.title: Rescue: River Deep, Mountain High
programme.desc: On Croagh Patrick in Co Mayo, the Mayo Mountain Rescue Team gears up for the annual religious pilgrimage, where up to 30,000 people set out to reach the summit of the mountain. More than 120 volunteers from other rescue teams, the Order of Malta, Civil Defence and Irish Air Corps assist the many injured pilgrims as they fall, have heart attacks and break ankles. On the Kinder Plateau, the Buxton and Kinder Mountain Rescue teams are called to an injured walker, while in the Lake District, three mountain rescue teams attend an injured ice climber with spinal and head injuries(n)

Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot(n)

programme.title: The Chase

Phil, Beckie, Kathy and Af work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught(n)

programme.title: Rescue: River Deep, Mountain High
programme.desc: On Croagh Patrick in Co Mayo, the Mayo Mountain Rescue Team gears up for the annual religious pilgrimage, where up to 30,000 people set out to reach the summit of the mountain. More than 120 volunteers from other rescue teams, the Order of Malta, Civil Defence and Irish Air Corps assist the many injured pilgrims as they fall, have heart attacks and break ankles. On the Kinder Plateau, the Buxton and Kinder Mountain Rescue teams are called to an injured walker, while in the Lake District, three mountain rescue teams attend an injured ice climber with spinal and head injuries(n)

It appears to be an open-and-shut case, but Superintendent Spence begins to question the man's guilt and invites Poirot to help uncover the identity of the real killer. The Belgian sleuth teams up with crime writer Ariadne Oliver (My Family's Zoe Wanamaker) as he tries to crack the case. Whodunit, starring David Suchet, with Richard Hope, Joe Absolom, Amanda Root, Sian Phillips and Raquel Cassidy(n)

credits.actor: David Suchet, Richard Hope, Joe Absolom, Amanda Root, Sian Phillips, Raquel Cassidy

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Lord Ashfordly's staff come under suspicion when some African carvings are stolen from the hall - but then an African chieftain arrives claiming the very same pieces belong to his tribe. Vernon sees a business opportunity that needs the royal stamp of approval and probationary PC Tom Nicholson turns up at the station. Nostalgic drama, starring Jason Durr, Rupert Vansittart and Geoffrey Hughes(n)

credits.actor: Jason Durr, Rupert Vansittart, Geoffrey Hughes

programme.desc: Annie is still keeping quiet about her mystery meal. Nick finds it hard to relax without Alice around. Chris sacks Tony Jarvis(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Schoolgirl Jennifer Sutcliffe becomes embroiled in a mystery when priceless jewels are hidden among her belongings. The precious stones once belonged to a prince who fell foul of a revolution, and now his cousin is a pupil at the same school as Jennifer, where Poirot finds himself drawn into the unlikely situation and realises nothing is quite what it seems. David Suchet stars as the Belgian sleuth, with Harriet Walter and Claire Skinner(n)

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: In the aftermath of the Abbey's eventful party, Mary is forced to answer a pressing question about her future, while Carson reveals surprisingly intimate details about his former life. Rose's trip to the bright lights of London sees her risking the Crawleys' reputation, and Gregson shares ambitious plans with Edith, taking their relationship to another level. Costume drama, starring Maggie Smith, Hugh Bonneville and Elizabeth McGovern(n)

credits.actor: Maggie Smith, Hugh Bonneville, Elizabeth McGovern

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: Jessica enters the world of haute couture to solve a series of murders committed in the seedy surroundings of a Parisian sweatshop, and the lover of a model who helped her research a book is targeted by a killer in Paris. Angela Lansbury stars(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A socialite is found dead next to the swimming pool of her manor house - following a series of threats against her. Barnaby and Troy are forced to dive deep into a dark world of adultery and deceit in affluent Midsomer Malham when they investigate. Mystery, starring John Nettles and Daniel Casey, with Charles Collingwood, Ann Bell and Miles Richardson(n)

credits.actor: John Nettles, Daniel Casey, Charles Collingwood, Ann Bell, Miles Richardson

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Brooks and Devlin reopen a 25-year-old case when the bones of a young boy are found buried inside the wall of a communal basement. The victim's former best friend takes part in therapy to revisit her memory of the night he disappeared and reveals a shocking secret, but Phillips and Steel worry her claims will not convince a jury. Guest starring Keith Barron and Colin Salmon, with Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber, Freema Agyeman and Ben Daniels(n)

credits.actor: Keith Barron, Colin Salmon, Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber, Freema Agyeman, Ben Daniels

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: An arson attack on a Turkish social club leaves 17 people dead and Brooks, Devlin and Chandler fearing the crime was racially motivated. They uncover a trail of false identities, but face opposition from a respected barrister when they need to remove a key piece of evidence from a suspect's leg. Meanwhile, Steel is forced to confront one of his oldest friends. Guest starring Charles Kay, with Bradley Walsh and Jamie Bamber(n)

credits.actor: Charles Kay, Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The detective's friend Sir Charles Cartwright (guest star Martin Shaw) hosts a cocktail party at his home in Cornwall, where an amiable local priest chokes to death on his drink. There seems to be no motive for murder, and no trace of poison found in the victim's glass, but weeks later, when Sir Charles's doctor friend Sir Bartholomew Strange dies in similar circumstances, he and Poirot team up to investigate. Drama, with Art Malik, Jane Asher and Anna Carteret, and David Suchet in the title role(n)

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: Change is in the air at the Abbey as an exciting opportunity for Alfred creates tension between Ivy and Daisy. Isobel appeals to Violet's sense of charity, but it doesn't come without a fight, while Branson considers a new future for himself and baby Sybbie. Raquel Cassidy (Teachers) joins the cast of the costume drama, with Matt Milne, Cara Theobold, Sophie McShera, Penelope Wilton, Maggie Smith and Allen Leech(n)

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: The beleaguered Cabot Cove residents fall victim to a callous development scheme - all except Jessica, who resolves to get to the bottom of the mystery. Angela Lansbury stars as the amateur sleuth, with William Windom, Ron Masak and Michael Horton(n)

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: When psychologist Reuben Beatty is murdered while moonlighting as a psychic, Lewis and Hathaway struggle to unravel the victim's baffling double life. Dark forces appear to be at work, and not just in the afterlife. Sanjeev Bhaskar guest stars, with Kevin Whately, Laurence Fox, Clare Holman and Rebecca Front(n)

credits.actor: Kevin Whately, Laurence Fox, Clare Holman, Rebecca Front

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: The unassuming detective launches an investigation when a conscientious objector dies while in police custody, and the judge who ordered his arrest is nearly killed in his booby-trapped home. His inquiries uncover a web of lies and corruption in the heart of wartime Hastings. Crime drama, starring Michael Kitchen and David Tennant(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Phyllis is jealous of Percy's involvement with Olive. Ken takes the Redmans to Brittany. Des tells Vera he is going to have to tell the truth in court(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Part two of two. Carl Lomax's case comes to court, and a local reporter soon rakes up the dirt over Tricia's controversial decision to supply his girlfriend with birth-control drugs. programme.title: Murder, She Wrote

programme.desc: Stan gets a new uniform and is proud to wear it - until he sits on a freshly painted chair. Classic comedy, starring Reg Varney(n)

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: Eagle-eyed George sees an investment opportunity when Mildred suggests their dog should have puppies. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy and Yootha Joyce(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Millionaire Gordon Cloade's wife is widowed for the second time at the tender age of 23, arousing the suspicions of his impoverished relatives. Fanning the flames of envy and hatred, psychic aunt Kathy Cloade reveals the grieving widow's first husband is in fact still alive, and a second death prompts Poirot to delve deeper into the family's dark secrets. David Suchet stars, with Jenny Agutter, Tim Piggot-Smith and Celia Imrie(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Aidensfield residents are shocked when an MI5 agent turns up in the village showing more than just a passing interest in the activities of Vernon Scripps and his mysterious associate, a man who disembarked from a Russian trawler docked in Whitby harbour. It seems there is an even darker side to the roguish wheeler-dealer than they suspected(n)

programme.desc: Rachel and Michael have an uncomfortable meeting. Chris catches one of his drivers behaving suspiciously. Joe asks Lisa if she is serious about Mark(n)

programme.desc: Chris does not know what to do about Tony Jarvis, the suspected drug smuggler, and warns Kathy to keep her nose out of his business. Mark wants Lisa to meet Annie(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Emily finds her old walking boots and decides to join a rambling club. Reg is depressed as Maureen is adamant they should finish. Vera and Jack go to court(n)

programme.desc: Masked gunmen burst into Bettabuy while Reg is at lunch with Maureen. Bet and Rita leave for Tenerife, and Sally feels used as Vera keeps dumping Tom on her(n)

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: An extravagant party at the Abbey gives the Crawleys an opportunity to reunite with old friends. Some guests, however, prove more welcome than others, and alongside the celebrations come skulduggery and heartache. Includes guest appearances by Former EastEnders heart-throb Nigel Harman (Dennis Rickman) and soprano Kiri Te Kanawa, who plays the role of real-life singer Dame Nellie Melba(n)

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: Jessica discovers a link between the death of a film fan and the supposed suicide of a leading Hollywood director 35 years previously. Murder mystery, starring Angela Lansbury and John Astin(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The detective investigates when a victim of fraud is found murdered on a golf course - but his inquiries are complicated by the discovery of a tramp's mutilated body nearby. To make matters worse, beautiful singer Bella Duveen is also around, seemingly intent on helping herself to a grim souvenir. David Suchet stars, with Hugh Fraser, Bill Moody and Jacinta Mulcahy(n)

programme.title: Unforgotten
programme.desc: Cassie and Sunny dig deeper into the lives of two of the people mentioned in the diary. They begin with Father Robert Greaves, who remembers Jimmy fondly, but is unable to help identify the mysterious 'Jo-Jo', the person who it appears gave Jimmy the journal. The detectives also meet Eric Slater, the former bookkeeper to Arlingham House. He also remembers the victim well - but can he provide any clues to his disappearance? Crime drama, starring Nicola Walker, Sanjeev Bhaskar, Bernard Hill and Tom Courtenay(n)

credits.actor: Nicola Walker, Sanjeev Bhaskar, Bernard Hill, Tom Courtenay

programme.title: Unforgotten
programme.desc: As the investigation into the cold-case murder continues, the forensic team discovers that the marks on the victim's body are consistent with known gangland torture methods. With this in mind, Cassie and Sunny question Sir Phillip about his involvement with a prominent East End crime family 40 years ago. Did the businessman or any of the shady associates of his youth have anything to do with Jimmy's disappearance or murder? Meanwhile, Eric and Claire's anniversary gets under way. Crime drama, starring Nicola Walker, Sanjeev Bhaskar, Trevor Eve and Tom Courtenay(n)

credits.actor: Nicola Walker, Sanjeev Bhaskar, Trevor Eve, Tom Courtenay

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: A former vice cop is found beaten to death, setting Brooks and Devlin off on an investigation through London's seedy underbelly. The victim's widow and his former boss both seem to know more about the murder than they are letting on - but the trail eventually leads to a respectable children's clothes shop. Crime drama, with guest appearances by Sean Pertwee (Cold Feet), Lesley Manville (Cranford) and Juliet Aubrey (Primeval)(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: The discovery of a body in a shallow grave by the Thames prompts Brooks and Devlin to reopen an eight-year-old murder case, casting doubt on a conviction made by Steel at the time. When the imprisoned man, Luke Slade, is given leave to appeal, he represents himself in court - but what starts as a legal battle soon develops into a personal vendetta. Crime drama, guest starring Iain Glen(n)

programme.title: Licence to Kill
programme.desc: James Bond plans revenge on a drug baron who maimed his friend. programme.title: Lethal Weapon
programme.desc: Cole juggles the realities of his job and his duty to be a good parent, taking his daughter to work as he and Murtaugh unite to foil a gang running a lottery scam. Away from the case, Roger receives a surprise visit from his father-in-law and it is not long before the pair clash. Action drama, starring Damon Wayans and Seann William Scott(n)

programme.title: Billy Connolly's Tracks Across America
programme.desc: In Texas, the comedian visits the stadium where soccer moms, ambition and marching bands collide. In Louisiana, he braves an attack from jumping Asian carp and meets the frog princesses of Rayne. After paying his respects at the graveside of musician Hank Williams, he ventures to Alabama, to hear the stories of the child crusaders who changed the course of history and segregation in the South. In Baltimore, Billy uncovers the tale of the multimillionaire who became known as the Mother of CSI before heading to his final destination of New York City(n)

programme.title: Football Genius
programme.desc: Stand-up comedian Tim Vine hosts the comedy quiz, in which two teams of football supporters, managed by The Chase's Paul Sinha and Olympic Gold medallist Sam Quek, play against each other in a game of virtual football by answering football-related questions. This match features two of the biggest clubs in English football as Paul's team of Arsenal fans go head to head with Sam's Liverpool side for the Football Genius trophy(n)

programme.title: People Like Pus: Zit ER
programme.desc: Behind the scenes at a specialist clinic where four dermatologists treat a range of skin conditions, often for patients who have been unable to get treatment on the NHS. programme.title: Cricket on 5
programme.desc: England v Australia. Action from day three of the Second Test at Lord's. England struggled with bat and ball here in the 2015 Ashes, and in the first innings were bowled out for 312 in reply to Australia's total of 566/8 declared. Alastair Cook top scored with 96, and Ben Stokes recorded an impressive 87 off 128 deliveries, but they were the only batsmen to surpass 50 and five of their teammates failed to reach double figures(n)

programme.title: Draining Alcatraz: The Impossible Escape
programme.desc: A look at the floor of San Francisco Bay surrounding the infamous prison, revealing why the damage caused by previous earthquakes helped make the island impossible to escape(n)

programme.title: Celebrity 5 Go Barging
programme.desc: After a death-defying crossing of the Edstone aqueduct, the longest cast iron example in England, Michael Buerk and Shaun Williamson visit a gin distillery to make genuine Elizabethan hooch, while Anita Harris goes foraging for herbs and flavours. Then they meet back at the barges for a gin-tasting session, pitching Shaun's 'Bottom's Up' against Michael's 'Goodnight Sweet Prince'. Then, alongside fellow travellers John Prescott and Amanda Barrie they head up to Stratford and journey's end at the Royal Shakespeare Theatre, where Shaun can finally achieve his life's ambition of playing Stratford(n)

programme.title: Cher: The Greatest Showgirl
programme.desc: Intercut with dramatised extracts, this documentary relives the meteoric highs and devastating lows of the singer and actress's career in showbusiness. It lifts the lid on the crucial events and key players in her life - from abandonment by a drug-addict father and her time in an orphanage as a baby, to her mother's frustrated acting career with a revolving door of husbands. Cher's relationship with Sonny Bono is also explored - a longed-for father figure whose controlling ways almost broke her(n)

programme.title: Doctor Dolittle
programme.desc: A Victorian veterinary surgeon is certified insane after developing the ability to talk to his patients in their own language. Undaunted, he manages to escape from the asylum and embarks on a quest to locate a rare aquatic snail. Oscar-winning musical comedy, with Rex Harrison, Samantha Eggar, Richard Attenborough and Anthony Newley. Songs include Talk to the Animals and I've Never Seen Anything Like It(n)

programme.title: Ever After: A Cinderella Story

Luckily, Renaissance genius Leonardo da Vinci is on hand to play fairy godfather and bring the pair together. programme.title: Catching History's Criminals: The Forensics Story
programme.desc: Gabriel Weston explores the forensic advances that have elevated murder weapons from mere instruments to crucial pieces of evidence. She sees how arsenic, the undetectable weapon of choice in the 19th century, was exposed as the killer thanks to one simple chemical test, and finds out how distinctive marks on a victim's skull led to the weapon and, in turn, the killer. Gabriel also looks at the latest forensic developments, from 3-D laser scanning that can reconstruct the sequence of events at the scene of a gun crime to the pioneering chemistry able to determine where in the world someone has been based on just a few strands of their hair(n)

programme.title: The Hunt for Hitler's Scientists
programme.desc: An examination of the Nazi weapons salvaged by the Allies at the end of the Second World War, and how the acquisition of enemy technology influenced future military campaigns(n)

programme.title: Time Team
programme.desc: Tony Robinson and the team investigate Gateholm Island, off the coast of Pembrokeshire, where a number of mysterious artefacts were discovered some time ago, suggesting the site was once of religious importance. The only way to get to the island is by rigging a 500-metre zip wire way above the wave-lashed rocks. The archaeologists battle inclement weather to begin a dig on the mainland, where team leader Francis Pryor sees characteristics suggestive of a classic Iron Age fort beginning to emerge(n)

programme.title: Time Team
programme.desc: Tony Robinson and the team descend on Bitterley, Shropshire, where local headmistress Jane Bishop has been leading an exploration of a field, which locals believe used to be the site of houses and streets. The villagers begin to excavate test pits in their own gardens to help the professionals, but it is three days before their answer is revealed(n)

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: Laurence Olivier narrates the story of the D-Day landings in June 1944, when the Allies launched the biggest ever amphibious invasion on the beaches of Normandy. Although both sides suffered heavy casualties, it proved to be the turning point in the war against Germany(n)

programme.title: Forbidden History
programme.desc: Jamie Theakston looks at the life and work of Serbian inventor Nikola Tesla, investigating conspiracy theories that the scientific establishment and national governments covered up his work on energy sources. He also finds out why so little evidence of his experiments remains today(n)

programme.title: Walter Tull - Forgotten Hero
programme.desc: Former EastEnders actor Nick Bailey investigates the story of Walter Tull, the pioneering black British footballer who died heroically fighting in World War One. He examines how Walter became the first black officer in the British Army when regulations required men of pure European descent, asks why he was denied the honour of a Military Cross for heroism and explores how he overcame racial abuse(n)

programme.title: A Very British Murder with Lucy Worsley
programme.desc: In the second programme, the historian explores how science and detection influenced the popular culture of murder during Victorian times. Writers including Charles Dickens and Wilkie Collins were fascinated by grisly crimes, and the literary genre that came out of it captured the imagination of readers. The presenter also reveals that when Jack the Ripper began his reign of terror in London at the same time Robert Louis Stevenson's Dr Jekyll and Mr Hyde was published, the idea of the serial killer was introduced to the British public(n)

programme.title: Time Team
programme.desc: Tony Robinson and the team visit the village of Beadnell on the Northumbrian coast, where mysterious fragments of human bone have emerged over recent years. Legend ties the area to local seventh-century Saint Ebbe, and it is widely believed that a 13th-century chapel once stood here. As the diggers set out to investigate possible remains of an earlier structure on the site, they unearth some shocking finds(n)

programme.title: Time Team
programme.desc: Tony Robinson and the team head to Swansea to investigate the history of White Rock, which was one of the first copper-smelting works. As the archaeologists strip turf and shift tons of earth, they reveal the traces of a once-great industry and rediscover the story of the men who worked in it(n)

programme.title: The Life of Mammals
programme.desc: David Attenborough examines the hunting tactics of the world's carnivores. Arctic foxes in the frozen north must collect food during warmer times to survive the winter, while the big cats of the southern hemisphere rely on stealth and surprise to catch each meal. The presenter also reveals how leopards and tigers are solitary hunters, coming together only to mate, whereas lions stay in family groups so they can tackle larger prey and better protect their young(n)

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: Tim Betts, the CEO of Muscle Maker Grill, goes undercover in the company he recently bought. Tim wants to make sure the brand is healthy and continues to expand, and he believes it's key that as they grow, each restaurant branch follows the same set of corporate standards. Tim thinks it's the perfect time to learn what's working and what needs an overhaul, so he meets staff including franchisees, a delivery driver, a food truck manager and a cashier whose flirtatious manner shocks him(n)

programme.title: Animal Rescue Live: Supervet Special
programme.desc: Animal Rescue Live continues its quest to support the work of rescue shelters across the UK. The team plays host to a dragon, and Made in Chelsea's animal enthusiast Jamie Laing drops in, adding his weight to the campaign to raise much-needed funds for thousands of homeless animals(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Danni Menzies helps a Solihull woman looking for a holiday home in La Mata on Spain's Costa Blanca, showing her five properties suitable for her £140,000 budget(n)

programme.title: Posh Pawnbrokers
programme.desc: At Pickwicks in London, boss Nathan and his right-hand woman Debbs do deals on a pair of original fish paintings, a mammoth antique Japanese jardinière and an original theatre organ with a £35,000 asking price. At London Road pawnbrokers in Sheffield, owner Dan and manageress Ellie take in a medieval coin that could be over 700 years old, some Indian bangles and four gold bars(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Hotel duty manager Andrew Carruthers hosts the first dinner party from Cumbria, where he hopes to impress his fellow diners with his hotel-themed evening, complete with five-star quality food including 'early reservation fishcakes' and a 'do not disturb truffle' in an attempt to win the £1,000 prize(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Fitness fanatic Jonny Thompson hosts the second night in Cumbria, where he hopes to wow his guests with a meaty menu - and they get to see a lot more of him than they had bargained for(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Eccentric sheepdog trainer Katy Cropper hosts the third dinner party in Cumbria, where she prepares 'come a cropper' prawns al fresco, opens a bottle of Champagne with a Masai sword, and serves some potent home-made horseradish sauce. Aside from the food, a sheep-herding demonstration proves to be a hit with her guests(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Holly Venning hosts the fourth dinner party from Cumbria and the food marketing manager wants to show her love for her home county through her menu. programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Estate agent Wendy Tinnion helms the final event in Cumbria and decides to throw a Friday night disco for her fellow diners. However, her guests are in for a shock when they discover something unexpected about their host(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun

programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.title: Trust Me, I'm a Doctor
programme.desc: Chris van Tulleken aims to sort fact from fiction as he investigates whether garlic, watermelon and beetroot really deserve the reputation they have gained as 'superfoods'. Michael Mosley also visits leading nutrition experts to find out whether sugar is really as toxic as tobacco, as had been suggested by recent studies. Plus, Dr Saleyha Ahsan explodes some of the myths surrounding the best way to treat burns(n)

programme.title: Mud Men
programme.desc: Steve Brooker and Johnny Vaughan search for hidden treasures along the shore in Greenwich, south-east London. They discover how the Tudors managed to stay sober despite drinking all day, and why beer bottles were thought to ward off evil witches(n)

programme.desc: Zilpah Hartley helps a newlywed couple find their perfect Caribbean retreat in the Cayman Islands. Husband John has built and designed his own home in the past, and brings his architectural know-how to bear on the assortment of apartments, plantation houses and studios they inspect(n)

programme.title: Kate and William: Inside the Royal Wedding
programme.desc: Documentary focusing on preparations for Prince William and Kate Middleton's wedding at Westminster Abbey on April 29, 2011. The programme includes details of the bride's dress and the couple's honeymoon plans, as well as interviews with friends, old acquaintances from school and former royal staff(n)

programme.title: I'm a Stranger

To discover what has become of it and acquire his rightful fortune, he needs the help of a detective, a window cleaner - and movie star Greta Gynt. programme.title: Trust Me, I'm a Doctor
programme.desc: With electronic cigarettes increasing in popularity, presenter Michael Mosley considers whether they are safe to use. The benefits of caffeine in helping people stay alert are also explored, as well as alternatives to the chemical. Chris van Tulleken investigates whether household chores can legitimately count as exercise, while Saleyha Ahsan offers first aid tips to treat someone suffering from hypothermia. programme.title: The Queen's Palaces
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce explores the stories behind official royal residences, which act as family homes and places to conduct the business of monarchy, and charts the evolution of their architecture. She begins with Buckingham Palace and learns about its links to London sewers, its features including state rooms and a home for camels and elephants, plus how its chandeliers are cleaned(n)

programme.title: Silk
programme.desc: Martha's loyalty is tested as she defends Jody Farr, the man responsible for the death of her former client Brendan Kay. However, when she suspects he is not being given a fair trial, she puts her personal feelings to one side and sets a dangerous trap to uncover the truth. Clive focuses all his efforts on the prosecution in a bid to forget his forthcoming tribunal, while Billy faces an uncertain future - no thanks to dodgy solicitor Micky. programme.title: Daddy Daycare
programme.desc: Nine men with limited childcare experience, who are either fathers or hope to have children soon, try to improve their skills by working in nurseries, taking on a variety of tasks such as nappy changing and supervising playtime. programme.title: Traders: Millions by the Minute
programme.desc: First of a two-part documentary going inside the fast and fiercely competitive world of financial trading, meeting the men and women who play the markets in big cities around the world. New York-based Karen juggles two sets of twins, a busy social calendar and a $200million hedge fund. Bob has spent more than three decades jostling for position on Chicago's cattle futures trading floor, while London day traders Will and Piers use their expertise to train others in the art of making money from tiny moves in the markets. Narrated by Saskia Reeves(n)

programme.title: Blood Feud

Crime drama, starring Paul King and Micky Curate(n)

programme.title: The Truth About
programme.desc: In the first of three documentaries focusing on health matters, Angela Rippon, who lost her mother to Alzheimer's, presents the latest scientific research about dementia - of which Alzheimer's is the most common form. She undergoes tests to see if she has any early signs, learns some surprising ways that may help prevent dementia and visits a number of people living with the disease. She also meets families that carry a gene for early onset Alzheimer's, discovering how they could be the best hope of finding a cure for this devastating disease(n)

programme.desc: News headlines, travel, weather and debate from across the capital(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Property expert Amanda Lamb helps Michael Grant search for a holiday home in Fuerteventura on the Canary Islands for £60,000. Properties on offer include a bargain flat, a traditional farmhouse and rural accommodation in the desert(n)

programme.title: Back from the Dead: The John Darwin Story
programme.desc: The story of John and Anne Darwin, who were sentenced to more than six years' imprisonment for an insurance fraud which saw the husband fake his own death in March 2002 and start a new life in Panama. The scheme was uncovered when John appeared at a police station in 2007 claiming to be a missing person suffering from amnesia(n)

programme.title: Make Bradford British
programme.desc: The sociological experiment concludes with the participants being paired up to learn about each other's backgrounds. A black man shows a retired policeman the estate where he once suffered a racially motivated attack, and a landlady invites a Muslim woman to work in a pub where the locals challenge her over her traditional attire. A welder comes away with a changed view of Islam after visiting one of the city's 80 mosques, while a self-confessed feminist invites taxi driver Mohammed to a classic British dinner party and questions him about his cultural views on women(n)

programme.title: Bonded By Blood 2: The Next Generation
programme.desc: A gangland shooting leaves the London underworld with a dangerous power vacuum. A thriving drug kingpin, a petty thief striving to be accepted and an unstable criminal are all determined to seize that power for themselves, tearing their friendship apart in the process. Crime drama, starring Sam Strike, Josh Myers and George Russo(n)

credits.actor: Sam Strike, Josh Myers, George Russo

programme.title: Honeytrap
programme.desc: A Brixton teenager's misguided attempts to attract the attention of a heartless wannabe rapper have terrible results. Drama, starring Jessica Sula, Lucien Laviscount and Ntonga Mwanza(n)

credits.actor: Jessica Sula, Lucien Laviscount, Ntonga Mwanza
